_notes: Source: hemas-patient (hospital enterprise). Target: US Core patient (US realm interoperability). Source has mandatory name, telecom, address, managingOrganization and hospital-specific extensions. Target adds USCDI extensions (race, ethnicity, etc.), has no named identifier slices, relaxes most mandatory cardinalities, and removes hospital-specific contact/communication constraints.

drifts.description: Source constraints removed: [hemas-pat-1 (error: Patient must have at least one identifier with a system), hemas-pat-2 (warning: Patient should have an emergency contact)]. US Core patient has no root-level constraints.
Optional extension registrationDate[0..1 mustSupport=true] present in source is removed in target.
Optional extension vipStatus[0..1 mustSupport=true] present in source is removed in target.
Optional extension insuranceProvider[0..* mustSupport=true] present in source is removed in target.
New extension race[0..1 mustSupport=false] added in target (USCDI v1 annotation). Source does not define this extension.
New extension ethnicity[0..1 mustSupport=false] added in target (USCDI v1 annotation). Source does not define this extension.
New extension tribalAffiliation[0..* mustSupport=false] added in target. Source does not define this extension.
New extension birthsex[0..1 mustSupport=false] added in target. Source does not define this extension.
New extension sex[0..1 mustSupport=false] added in target (USCDI annotation). Source does not define this extension.
New extension genderIdentity[0..* mustSupport=false] added in target (USCDI annotation). Source does not define this extension.

Identifier slicing removed: source has named slices [hospitalId[1..1 mustSupport=true], nic[0..1 mustSupport=true]]; US Core has no named identifier slices (only general sub-element constraints at identifier.system and identifier.value level).
Source mandatory identifier slice hospitalId[1..1 mustSupport=true] removed in target. No equivalent named slice in US Core patient.
Source optional identifier slice nic[0..1 mustSupport=true] removed in target. No equivalent named slice in US Core patient.
active cardinality relaxed and MustSupport removed: source [1..1 mustSupport=true] → target [0..1 mustSupport=false] (US Core does not constrain active). Min decreased and MS removed.
name.family cardinality relaxed: source [1..1 mustSupport=true] → target [0..1 mustSupport=true] (US Core no explicit min, inherits R4 base [0..1]). Min decreased. MustSupport preserved.
name.given cardinality relaxed: source [1..* mustSupport=true] → target [0..* mustSupport=true] (US Core no explicit min, inherits R4 base [0..*]). Min decreased. MustSupport preserved.

telecom cardinality relaxed and MustSupport removed from required element: source [1..* mustSupport=true] → target [0..* mustSupport=false] (US Core does not constrain telecom cardinality or element-level MS). Source sub-element constraints on system and value are preserved in US Core.
New telecom.use MustSupport=true with required binding added in target: US Core adds telecom.use[mustSupport=true required:contact-point-use]. Source does not constrain telecom.use.
birthDate cardinality relaxed: source [1..1 mustSupport=true] → target [0..1 mustSupport=true] (US Core no explicit min, inherits R4 base [0..1]). Min decreased. MustSupport preserved.
address cardinality relaxed: source [1..* mustSupport=true] → target [0..* mustSupport=true] (US Core no explicit min). Min decreased. MustSupport preserved.
address.line cardinality relaxed: source [1..* mustSupport=true] → target [0..* mustSupport=true] (US Core no explicit min). Min decreased. MustSupport preserved.
address.city cardinality relaxed: source [1..1 mustSupport=true] → target [0..1 mustSupport=true] (US Core no explicit min). Min decreased. MustSupport preserved.
New MustSupport=true with new binding added on address.state in target: extensible(http://hl7.org/fhir/us/core/ValueSet/us-core-usps-state). Source does not constrain address.state.
New address.postalCode[mustSupport=true] added in target. Source does not constrain address.postalCode. Optional element; MS added.

address.country MustSupport removed from required element and cardinality relaxed: source [1..1 mustSupport=true] → target not constrained (US Core does not define address.country, inherits R4 base [0..1 mustSupport=false]). Min decreased and MS removed.
address.district MustSupport removed: source mustSupport=true (optional element); US Core does not constrain address.district. MS removed from optional element.
maritalStatus MustSupport removed: source mustSupport=true extensible:http://hl7.org/fhir/ValueSet/marital-status; US Core does not constrain maritalStatus. MS removed from optional element.

Source contact constraints removed: source has contact[mustSupport=true] with relationship[1..* mustSupport=true extensible:patient-contactrelationship], name[1..1 mustSupport=true], telecom[1..* mustSupport=true]. US Core does not constrain contact at all.

communication MustSupport removed at element level: source mustSupport=true; US Core mustSupport=false on the communication element itself (though language sub-element remains mustSupport=true in both).

communication.language binding changed: source extensible(http://hl7.org/fhir/ValueSet/languages) → target extensible(http://hl7.org/fhir/us/core/ValueSet/simple-language). Different value sets, same binding strength.
generalPractitioner reference target removed: source constrains to hemas/Practitioner; US Core does not constrain generalPractitioner (no target profile).
generalPractitioner MustSupport removed: source mustSupport=true; US Core does not constrain generalPractitioner. MS removed from optional element.

managingOrganization MustSupport removed from required element and cardinality relaxed: source [1..1 mustSupport=true →hemas/Organization]; US Core does not constrain managingOrganization (inherits R4 base [0..1 mustSupport=false]). Min decreased and MS removed.
managingOrganization reference target removed: source constrains to hemas/Organization; US Core does not constrain managingOrganization (no target profile).
